verses.commentary: Apostles are servants of Christ and stewards of God's mysteries. The role is servant, not celebrity; the trust is divine mysteries, not personal kingdoms.
Stewards must prove faithful. The qualification is reliability in handling what belongs to another, not personal success or prominence.
Paul cares very little about human judgment or even self judgment. True evaluation comes from God alone, freeing Paul from people pleasing.
A clear conscience doesn't establish innocence. The Lord judges, and his assessment alone matters. Self acquittal means nothing.
Don't judge before the appointed time. The Lord will expose hidden things and reveal heart motives. Then each receives praise from God.
Paul applies these things to himself and Apollos so they learn not to go beyond what is written. The goal is preventing prideful faction formation.
Who makes you different? What do you have that you didn't receive? If received, why boast as though you didn't? Grace eliminates grounds for pride.
Already full, already rich, already reigning without the apostles. Paul's sarcasm exposes their delusional self assessment and premature triumphalism.
God displayed apostles last, like those condemned to die in the arena. Apostleship means spectacle to the universe, angels, and humans.
Fools for Christ versus wise in Christ. Weak versus strong. Dishonored versus honored. Paul's irony highlights the contrast between apostolic reality and Corinthian pretension.
To this hour: hungry, thirsty, poorly clothed, brutally treated, homeless. Present tense ongoing suffering marks authentic apostolic ministry.
Working with our own hands, blessing when cursed, enduring when persecuted. Apostolic response to mistreatment follows Christ's pattern.
Answering kindly when slandered, becoming the scum of the earth, the garbage of the world. The world's verdict on apostles reveals its values.
Paul writes not to shame but to warn as dear children. Correction comes from love, not desire to humiliate.
Ten thousand guardians in Christ, but not many fathers. Paul fathered them through the gospel, creating unique relationship.
Therefore imitate me. Paul can call for imitation because he imitates Christ. The request is bold but legitimate.
Timothy, Paul's beloved faithful son in the Lord, will remind them of Paul's ways in Christ, consistent with teaching everywhere.
Some became arrogant assuming Paul wouldn't come. Absence is not permission for pride or misconduct.
Paul will come soon if the Lord wills, finding out not how these arrogant people talk but what power they have. Words versus power will be tested.
God's kingdom is not a matter of talk but of power. Rhetoric and reality are distinct; the kingdom operates in power, not mere words.
What do you prefer: rod of discipline or love and gentle spirit? The choice of how Paul comes depends on their response.
